title: monopsis debilis var. debilis
intro: Monopsis debilis var. debilis, also known as Wild Jasmine or Poisonous Jasmine, is a plant species belonging to the family Saururaceae. The plant's leaves and roots are rich in medicinal properties, including antiseptic and anti-inflammatory compounds, which have been traditionally used to treat various health issues. Monopsis debilis var. debilis has been used to treat respiratory problems, such as bronchitis and asthma, due to its expectorant properties, and it also has been used to treat gastrointestinal issues, including diarrhea and nausea. The plant can be prepared as a herbal tea, tincture, or infusion, and it is often used in combination with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties.
